description: State transition without pre-condition check — should block
diff:
--- a/src/order.py
+++ b/src/order.py
@@ -20,6 +20,8 @@ class Order:
     def cancel(self):
-        if self.status == 'active':
-            self.status = 'cancelled'
-            self.refund()
+        self.status = 'cancelled'
+        self.refund()

mock_response.result: {"review_status":"completed","pass":false,"issues":[{"check":"State transition removed pre-condition check","verdict":"cancel() now sets status to cancelled without verifying current status is active","fix":"Restore the status == active guard before transitioning","severity":"critical","confidence":"high","category":"correctness","file":"src/order.py","line_hint":"L22","evidence":["Removed line: if self.status == 'active' guard was deleted","Added line: self.status = 'cancelled' now executes unconditionally","refund() called without verifying order is in cancellable state"],"what_would_falsify_this":"If cancel() is only reachable from a caller that already verifies active status","suggested_validation":"Add test: call cancel() on an already-cancelled order and verify it does not double-refund","abstain_condition":""}],"summary":"State transition guard removed — cancel() can now double-refund"}
